interactions.summary: Jiang describes Christian Zionist/pre-millennial dispensationalist end-times beliefs, the conditions around Israel, the Third Temple, return, Antichrist, millennium, and Al-Aqsa, then answers the mosque-destruction question by saying it would intentionally galvanize both Muslim opposition and antisemitism as part of the plan. The interviewer asks how much predictive weight this driver has.

claims.claim: Jiang says Christian Zionists believe God's plan can be accelerated by fulfilling end-times conditions around Israel, the Third Temple, return, war, and Jesus's return.
claims.temporal_scope: Description of Christian Zionist eschatology in this interview.

claims.claim: He claims Christian Zionist ideas are embedded in American political life and points to Peter Hegseth's Crusader symbolism and Third Temple talk as an example.
Jiang says destroying Al-Aqsa would serve extremist eschatology because it would push Jews toward Jerusalem and help create the world-against-Israel War of Gog and Magog.

signature_moments.moment: In Jiang's bleak reading, backlash is not a mistake in the eschatological plan; it is one of the plan's objectives.
signature_moments.source_phrase: destroying the Al-Aqsaq Mosque accomplishes both objectives
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It preserves the disturbing strategic logic of provocation.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Christian Zionist end-times framework in which believers can accelerate God's plan by helping fulfill scriptural conditions around Israel and the return of Jesus.

chronology_notes.note: This is a dated March 2026 claim that rumors and access changes around Al-Aqsa have predictive significance in Jiang's model.

uncertainty_notes.note: Jiang labels the Al-Aqsa demolition material as rumor/planned logic; treat as his prediction/speculation, not established event.
